item.title: Spain Announces Deal With Algeria to Increase Gas Imports
item.description: Algeria will increase gas supplies to Spain as global energy markets face heightened instability linked to the war in Iran, the Spanish government said.

item.title: Nvidia Sees First Bout of Retail-Investor Selling Since July
item.description: Individual investors have long piled into shares of Nvidia Corp., whose meteoric surge turned it into the biggest company in the S&P 500 Index. But on Wednesday they dumped the stock for the first time since July, signaling a potential shift as the Iran war pulls the market down from its AI-fueled rally.

item.title: Argentina Economy Expanded in Line With Expectations in January
item.description: Argentina’s economy posted a slight expansion in January following a quarter of weaker-than-expected growth for President Javier Milei.

item.title: SpaceX Lines Up April Investor Briefings as IPO Questions Swirl
item.description: SpaceX is telling prospective IPO investors to expect briefings in April from company executives, according to people familiar with the matter, as advisers scramble to file for potentially the biggest listing of all time.

item.title: Traders Hedge War Shock That Could Force Fed Hike Within Weeks
item.description: Bond traders, spooked by the prospect of the conflict in Iran escalating further, are looking to hedge against worst-case war outcomes that could force the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates in coming weeks.

item.title: Album-Centric Record Deals Come Under Growing Scrutiny
item.description: In a world of TikTok-driven singles, album-centric music deals are somehow hanging on.

item.title: Banxico Resumes Monetary Easing Despite Price Pressures
item.description: Mexico’s central bank resumed its monetary easing cycle after a brief pause, signaling concern over a weakening economy even as consumer inflation accelerates, driven by domestic pressures and the fallout from weeks of war in Iran.

item.title: Meta Increases Investment in El Paso Data Center to $10 Billion
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. will spend more than $10 billion to develop a data center in El Paso, Texas, a jump from prior projections and the latest in a series of major investments focused on the infrastructure needed to support its artificial intelligence ambitions.

item.title: Millennium and Engineers Gate Wind Down Hedge Fund Partnership
item.description: Millennium Management is in the process of taking back cash it gave to Engineers Gate to trade on its behalf, after the hedge funds agreed to end their more than three-year-old partnership.

item.title: Iran Has a Lesson in Economic Warfare to Offer Trump
item.description: Rising energy prices, shifting market expectations and Iran’s strategic leverage are reshaping the economic outlook — and testing US policy.

item.title: Apple Plans to Open Up Siri to Rival AI Assistants in iOS 27 Update
item.description: Apple Inc. plans to open Siri to outside artificial intelligence assistants, a major move aimed at bolstering the iPhone as an AI platform.

item.title: Congress Could Pass AI Standard in ‘Months,’ Key Trump Aide Says
item.description: White House adviser David Sacks said Congress could pass bipartisan artificial intelligence legislation within months, a move that would fulfill President Donald Trump’s pledge to create a national playbook for regulating the emerging technology.

item.title: Capital Group to Buy LA Headquarters Tower, Plans Charlotte Hub
item.description: Capital Group, the world’s largest active fund manager, is buying its current headquarters building in downtown Los Angeles as part of expansion plans that also include opening an office in Charlotte, North Carolina.

item.title: Oil Traders Fatigued by Wild Price Swings Throttle Back Flows
item.description: Almost a month into the war in Iran, oil traders reeling from massive market swings are pulling back as conflicting messaging from Washington DC and Tehran keeps prices gyrating.

item.title: European PE Giant Permira Looks to Buy Beaten Up Software Loans
item.description: Permira, the €85 billion ($98 billion) global private equity firm which lends to technology companies, is finding opportunity in software loans battered by fears of AI disruption.

item.title: New York City Shrinks Size of Mega Bond Deal Amid Market Tumult
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani just faced his administration’s first big test in the municipal-bond market amid growing concerns about the city’s finances.

item.title: Bank of England Rate-Setters Doubt Iran War Will Trigger UK Price Spiral
item.description: Bank of England rate-setters cast doubt over the threat of a price spiral from the Iran war on Thursday, even as markets expect three hikes from the central bank this year.

item.title: Warren Presses SEC on Venezuela Bond Trades Before Maduro Ouster
item.description: A pair of senators pressed the Securities and Exchange Commission and the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority about trading around the time of the US military action in Venezuela ousting President Nicolas Maduro.

item.title: Chip Security Bill Advances in House Following Super Micro Case
item.description: A House panel advanced legislation that would require the Commerce Department to demand chipmakers do more to keep AI technology from being smuggled to China, an effort that’s gained new urgency after last week’s indictment of a Super Micro Computer Inc. co-founder for allegedly diverting Nvidia Corp. processors to Chinese buyers.

item.title: Dutch Court Bans XAI’s Grok From Nonconsensual Undressing
item.description: An Amsterdam court banned Elon Musk’s xAI from generating and distributing undressed images of people in the Netherlands without permission, the latest in wider pushback against the controversial feature.

item.title: Musk Rips Twitter Verdict, Claims Jury’s $4.20 ‘Joke’ Mocked Him
item.description: Elon Musk’s lawyer says the billionaire didn’t get a fair trial because a San Francisco jury slipped a “bizarre and highly questionable” joke into a verdict form when it concluded he defrauded Twitter Inc. shareholders before acquiring the social media platform.

item.title: This Is Nothing Like Post-Liberation Day
item.description: We haven’t been here before.

item.title: BOC Says Oil Price Shock Will Push Near-Term Inflation Higher
item.description: The Bank of Canada’s second-in-command reiterated that policymakers see higher oil prices boosting inflation in the coming months, and said the central bank is reviewing how it communicates core measures of price pressures to the public.

item.title: Reform UK Says Big Tech Must Share ‘Pain’ of Fraud With Banks
item.description: Reform UK’s deputy leader said big technology companies must “share the pain” with banks of reimbursing the victims of financial fraud facilitated by their platforms.

item.title: JPMorgan Plans Private Credit Fund That Allows 7.5% Redemptions
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is planning a new fund investing in private credit that will allow investors to redeem 7.5% a quarter — and, potentially, offer monthly withdrawals — as the $1.8 trillion market grapples with an unprecedented liquidity squeeze.

item.title: Hertz, Avis Soar as Airport Chaos Drives Boom in Rental Demand
item.description: Travelers across the US are enduring seemingly interminable airport lines as a partial government shutdown strains staffing at the Transportation Security Administration, while investors are piling into car-rental companies on bets the disruption will push more people onto the road.

item.title: Bitcoin Faces $14 Billion Options Expiry While Middle East Turmoil Mounts
item.description: Bitcoin’s largest options expiry of the year is colliding with geopolitical volatility that shows no sign of letting up with make or break peace talks uncertain.

item.title: The Money Behind March Madness: Finances Fueling Top College Teams
item.description: Basketball teams’ financial health is more important than ever as schools can now pay their student-athletes.

item.title: Pernod Ricard Weighs Deal for Jack Daniel’s Maker Brown-Forman
item.description: Pernod Ricard SA is exploring a potential acquisition of Brown-Forman Corp., the owner of Jack Daniel’s whiskey, as alcoholic drink companies look at ways to consolidate amid an industry downturn, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Funding Markets Show Dash for Cash as Firms Build Buffers
item.description: Recent activity in funding markets shows a quiet push by financial institutions to build up buffers that would help protect against any credit meltdowns or market distress, a sign they perceive rising risks even as overall conditions remain stable for now.
